records.ety: [OE. wey, way, AS. weg; akin to OS., D., OHG., & G. weg, Icel. vegr, Sw. väg, Dan. vei, Goth. wigs, L. via, and AS. wegan to move, L. vehere to carry, Skr. vah. √136. Cf. Convex, Inveigh, Vehicle, Vex, Via, Voyage, Wag, Wagon, Wee, Weigh.]
usage: -- Way, Street, Highway, Road. Way is generic, denoting any line for passage or conveyance; a highway is literally one raised for the sake of dryness and convenience in traveling; a road is, strictly, a way for horses and carriages; a street is, etymologically, a paved way, as early made in towns and cities; and, hence, the word is distinctively applied to roads or highways in compact settlements.

defs: That by, upon, or along, which one passes or processes; opportunity or room to pass; place of passing; passage; road, street, track, or path of any kind; as, they built a way to the mine.
quotes: I shall him seek by way and eke by street.
The way seems difficult, and steep to scale.
The season and ways were very improper for his majesty's forces to march so great a distance.

defs: Length of space; distance; interval; as, a great way; a long way.
quotes: And whenever the way seemed long, Or his heart began to fail.
I prythee, now, lead the way.

defs: Course or direction of motion or process; tendency of action; advance.
quotes: If that way be your walk, you have not far.
And let eternal justice take the way.

defs: The means by which anything is reached, or anything is accomplished; scheme; device; plan.
quotes: My best way is to creep under his gaberdine.
By noble ways we conquest will prepare.
What impious ways my wishes took!

defs: Manner; method; mode; fashion; style; as, the way of expressing one's ideas.
Regular course; habitual method of life or action; plan of conduct; mode of dealing.
quotes: Her ways are ways of pleasantness, and all her paths are peace.
When men lived in a grander way.
The public ministers that fell in my way.

defs: Determined course; resolved mode of action or conduct; as, to have one's way.
Progress; as, a ship has way.
The timbers on which a ship is launched.
The longitudinal guides, or guiding surfaces, on the bed of a planer, lathe, or the like, along which a table or carriage moves.

quotes: All keep the broad highway, and take delight With many rather for to go astray.
There is but one road by which to climb up.
When night Darkens the streets, then wander forth the sons Of Belial, flown with insolence and wine.

defs: To go or travel to; to go in, as a way or path.
To move; to progress; to go.
quotes: On a time as they together wayed.
